title: Fix direct navigation 404 on Blazor sample pages
body:
GitHub Pages only serves the **root-level** `404.html` for all missing paths — subdirectory `404.html` files are never invoked. The existing SPA redirect handler lived at `docs/_site/sample/404.html` and was never reached, so direct navigation to any sample route (e.g. `/SkiaSharp.Extended/sample/shapes`) returned GitHub's default 404.

## Changes

- **`docs/404.html`** — new root-level 404 page with an inline SPA redirect script. Dynamically detects the `sample` segment in the URL path, then encodes the remaining route as a query string and redirects to that sample's `index.html`. Works for both main and staging deployments without hardcoded segment counts:
  - Main: `/SkiaSharp.Extended/sample/shapes` → `/SkiaSharp.Extended/sample/?/shapes`
  - Staging: `/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/42/sample/shapes` → `/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/42/sample/?/shapes`
  - Non-sample 404s fall through to a plain "Page Not Found" message.

- **`docs/docfx.json`** — added `404.html` to the `resource` files so docfx copies it to `_site/` (and thus to the gh-pages root) during every build.

- **`.github/workflows/docs-deploy.yml`** — two improvements to the staging workflow:
  - The `comment` job now also runs on `synchronize` events (new commits pushed to the PR), matching the deploy job behavior. The script updates an existing bot comment rather than skipping, so the staging preview link is always refreshed after each push.
  - The staging deploy step now copies `404.html` to the gh-pages root in addition to `staging/$PR_NUMBER/`. Because GitHub Pages only serves the root-level `404.html` for all missing paths, staging deep-links (e.g. `/SkiaSharp.Extended/staging/369/sample/shapes`) would otherwise never trigger the SPA redirect — they now work correctly as soon as a staging deployment completes.

The complementary decode script already in `sample/index.html` reads the encoded query string and restores the correct URL before the Blazor router initializes — no changes needed there.
